title: panicum crus-galli var. hispidulum
intro: Panicum crus-galli var. hispidulum, also known as Bristle Leaf Panic Grass, is a type of grass native to North America, characterized by its distinctive bristled leaves and stems. Traditionally, Bristle Leaf Panic Grass has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing swelling and pain, as well as supporting the immune system and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Bristle Leaf Panic Grass are often dried and used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
